ddls:
CREATE TABLE "transmissions" (
  "transmission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each transmission type */,
  "transmission_type" TEXT /* Type of transmission (e.g., human-to-human, animal-to-human) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the transmission type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("transmission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "symptoms" (
  "symptom_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each symptom */,
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* ID of the individual reporting the symptom */,
  "symptom_name" TEXT /* Name of the symptom (e.g., fever, cough) */,
  "symptom_date" TEXT /* Date the symptom was reported */,
  "severity_level" INTEGER /* Severity level of the symptom (e.g., 1-5) */,
  "symptom_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the symptom in days */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("symptom_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "treatments" (
  "treatment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each treatment */,
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* ID of the individual receiving the treatment */,
  "treatment_name" TEXT /* Name of the treatment (e.g., medication, hospitalization) */,
  "treatment_date" TEXT /* Date the treatment was administered */,
  "treatment_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the treatment in days */,
  "medication_name" TEXT /* Name of the medication used for treatment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("treatment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "outcomes" (
  "outcome_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each outcome */,
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* ID of the individual with the outcome */,
  "outcome_name" TEXT /* Name of the outcome (e.g., recovery, death) */,
  "outcome_date" TEXT /* Date the outcome occurred */,
  "reason_for_outcome" TEXT /* Reason for the outcome (e.g., recovery from treatment, death due to complications) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("outcome_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tests" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each test */,
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* ID of the individual being tested */,
  "test_name" TEXT /* Name of the test (e.g., PCR, antibody) */,
  "test_date" TEXT /* Date the test was administered */,
  "test_result" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating whether the test result was positive */,
  "test_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the test result */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., health official, researcher, admin) */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization the user belongs to */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the system was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the access event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "notification_message" TEXT /* Message contained in the notification */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., email, SMS) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "disease_outbreaks" (
  "outbreak_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each outbreak */,
  "disease_name" TEXT /* Name of the disease causing the outbreak */,
  "outbreak_date" TEXT /* Date when the outbreak started */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the outbreak (e.g., city, region) */,
  "outbreak_type" TEXT /* Type of outbreak (e.g., foodborne, waterborne) */,
  "transmission_type" TEXT /* Type of transmission (e.g., human-to-human, animal-to-human) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("outbreak_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "outbreak_contacts" (
  "contact_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each contact */,
  "outbreak_id" INTEGER /* ID of the outbreak the contact belongs to */,
  "individual_id" INTEGER /* ID of the individual who was contacted */,
  "contact_date" TEXT /* Date when the contact was made */,
  "contact_type" TEXT /* Type of contact (e.g., phone, email) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("contact_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_outbreak_contacts_outbreak_id FOREIGN KEY ("outbreak_id") REFERENCES disease_outbreaks ("outbreak_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "outbreak_locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "outbreak_id" INTEGER /* ID of the outbreak the location belongs to */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the location */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the location is */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the location is */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* Zip code of the location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_outbreak_locations_outbreak_id FOREIGN KEY ("outbreak_id") REFERENCES disease_outbreaks ("outbreak_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "disease_symptoms" (
  "disease_symptom_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each disease symptom */,
  "disease_name" TEXT /* Name of the disease */,
  "symptom_name" TEXT /* Name of the symptom */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("disease_symptom_id")
)
